message:
feat(conformance): add mock-based scenario execution with HostcallInterceptor

Expand the conformance scenario test suite (743 lines net) to support
deterministic testing of extensions that use exec/http/session hostcalls
via the new HostcallInterceptor trait:

MockSpecInterceptor:
- Implements HostcallInterceptor to provide canned responses for exec,
  http, and session hostcalls based on scenario fixture specifications
- Records all intercepted calls for post-hoc assertion checking
- Supports pattern matching on exec command names, HTTP URLs, and
  session message content

LoadedExtensionWithMocks:
- Extension loader variant that wires MockSpecInterceptor into the
  JsExtensionRuntimeHandle via start_with_interceptor()
- Enables full scenario execution without real shell, network, or
  terminal access

Scenario fixture extensions:
- ScenarioExpectation extended with mock_exec, mock_http, mock_session
  fields for specifying expected hostcall patterns and canned responses
- check_expectations_with_mocks() validates both return values and
  intercepted hostcall sequences

This enables conformance testing of extensions like git-checkpoint,
dirty-repo-guard, and diff that depend on exec hostcalls, running
entirely in CI sandboxes without real git repos or shell access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
